detail: acceptance 13/13 pass dev+prod; debug tests pass; validate contract enforced

analysis: from_torch(TILE) zero-pads (confirmed via source). w_non_aligned (D%32) is FREE: zero-padded D cols give 0 contribution in QKt contraction and 0 in PV free dim; output D-pad cols written as whole tiles, sliced by logical shape. h_non_aligned S_q FREE: padding Q rows produce finite discarded output, whole-tile write + logical slice. STRUCTURAL work = S_kv%32 padding: mask last-KV-tile padding cols to -inf (bf16 0xFF80) additively before row-max, only on last kv chunk (matches production SDPA generate_noncausal_padded_mask). Keeping _chunk_size divisor trick (whole chunks) so only the boundary tile of the last chunk is partial.

golden: 252 passed (212 prior + 40 new non-aligned), 2017 xfailed, 0 failed, 0 xpass — no SUPPORTED drift
regression: test_regression 9 failed = the SAME pre-existing precision misses (aligned adversarial shapes, R2 target); 0 new failures
decision: KEEP _chunk_size divisor trick: correct+DRY+general for all tested shapes; the coarse-chunk+partial-remainder replacement the verifier flagged as While here needs partial-CHUNK kernel machinery (variable matmul subblock n / reader-writer tile counts), benefits only prime Skv_t>4 shapes (NONE in any test), and risks the 212 aligned + perf-shape no-regression invariant. Deferred to Refinement 1b. Both NAMED axis values (w/h non_aligned) fully landed + golden-green => R1 [x].

note: Coarse chunk min(axis_t,4) + partial last chunk. All three shape builders (MatmulBlockShape/ReduceInputBlockShape/EltwiseShape) take runtime uint32 extents; matmul_block reissues Short init per call with runtime out_subblock_w, so runtime subblock decomposition is safe. Plan: thread sq_valid (per q-chunk) and skv_valid (per KV chunk) runtime tile counts through reader/compute/writer; derive N-subblock decomposition on device (DRY, drop host _matmul_subblocks CT args); keep CBs sized to full chunk.

Hang isolated: custom-mask + partial KV chunk of width 3 (4->3 out_subblock_w transition). Triage: TRISC2 packer stuck in are_packers_configured_correctly. Data: none+w3 passes, custom+w1/w2/w4 pass, custom+w3 hangs; phase-0 golden (2,3,192,96) with constant compile-time w3 passed. Conclusion: mm_block_init_short does not fully re-issue packer config on a mid-loop out_subblock_w change to a non-power-of-2 width.

Keep QKT out_subblock_w CONSTANT across the KV loop. has_partial_kv = Skv_t%Skv_chunk_t!=0: if partial, out_subblock_w=1 (safe constant); else optimal full-width decomp (perf shape untouched, Skv_t%4==0). Only in1_num_subblocks (loop count) varies per chunk.

Root cause (static analyzer): cb_scores ring-buffer straddle. In-place mask add rotates the read pointer by per-chunk tile count; when partial remainder skv_last does not divide Skv_chunk_t (2*skv_last>chunk, uniquely width 3 for chunk 4) the reduce read window crosses the ring wrap -> OOB unpack -> packer wedge. Fix: _chunk_size picks largest coarse chunk<=target whose remainder DIVIDES the chunk (rem|chunk <=> 2*rem<=chunk). Skv_t=101->4, Skv_t=7->3, Skv_t=6->4, Skv_t=296->4. All 22 coarse-chunk cases pass incl prev-hanging prime_cross custom + headline prime101.

[x] R1b landed. Golden 252 passed/2017 xfailed (0 fail, 0 xpass). Unit 62/62 prod mode. test_regression 9 fail (same R2-target precision)/30 pass unchanged. Coarse-chunk test 22/22 incl headline prime101 (Skv_t=101->chunk 4) + prime_cross custom (prev hang). Committed 3bab8f76ba.

summary: R2 = SUPPORTED dtype +={float32,bfloat8_b}, fp32_dest_acc_en+=False, EXCLUSION {float32,False}; intermediate cb_scores/cb_exp Float32 under fp32-DEST (bf16 otherwise so perf shape byte-identical). No compute-kernel change expected (all helpers). Thread real dtype bytes + interm format into _fit_l1. bf8b+custom mask (-inf in block-float) and bf8b+non-aligned are the risk cells to probe.

detail: fp32 intermediates fixed the 2 genuine-precision large_magnitude cases; remaining 7 (uniform/negative) are max_abs=1-bf16-ULP, ulp_p99=1, normalized-rms metric artifact on near-constant reference. bf16 OUTPUT quantization is the floor; regression tests hard-code bf16 so R2 float32 path cannot reach them. Documented not-a-bug, never green in prior phases, non-blocking (no_axes_found in verifier CLI).

golden 1181 passed/1088 xfailed/0 fail/0 xpass (was 252); perf loose 1x10x9472x128 passes (R3 unblocked); unit 334 passed/112 skipped (incl precision matrix 272/112); translated bf16 sanity green. R2 marked [x] full.

text: Reader uses per-tile read+barrier+push (double_buffer anti-pattern). Flagged shape 1x10x9472x128 re-reads K+V per q-chunk (~3.5GB total DRAM reads across 110 cores) -> reader is dominant & latency-bound. Lever: batch per-chunk async reads then one barrier (KV CBs already KV_DEPTH-deep). Gate full-chunk batching on !has_partial_kv (CB ring straddle-safe only for slot-aligned pushes). Apply symmetric writer twin gated on !has_partial_q (but reader dominates writes ~150x).

no-DM ablation (stub ALL reader NoC transfers, keep CB signaling+barrier): device FW = 11.01ms == baseline 11.06ms == batched 11.21ms. Reader DM is entirely hidden behind compute via existing KV_DEPTH=2 double-buffer. Flagged shape 1x10x9472x128 is COMPUTE-bound, not DM-bound. R3 DM lever (reader batching) is correct but off the critical path -> no wall-time win on this shape. Complementary step is compute-side (R5).

details: reader+writer batching applied+kept (no regression). baseline 11.064ms -> batched 11.007ms (flat). no-DM ablation 11.01ms proves compute-bound. golden 1181 pass/0 fail (== R2); unit 55/55; precision-matrix 272/112; guard 8/8. Marked [~], filed R3a (compute-side).

summary: Compute-bound flagged shape (1x10x9472x128, chunk 4). Lever: coarsen Sq/Skv chunk targets 4->8 (amortize ~10 per-chunk phases over 2x tiles; grow QKT matmul out_subblock_w 4->8 toward dest_limit=8). Then re-enable R3 DM batching + re-measure. Turn the _fit_l1 chunk-target knob (single source), rely on golden+unit regression net.

Coarsen target 4->8 gave +7% (9.666->9.007ms) but exposed latent regression: 6 golden cells on Q1x8x4096x128 GQA fail catastrophically (pcc=-0, rms=inf). Isolated: discriminator is partial_kv=True (not batching, not L1). L1 shrink 8->7 -> _chunk_size(128,7)=6 (partial); partial chunk + >1 wu/core (total_work=128>110) misaligns cb_scores ring across work units -> reduce read straddles wrap -> garbage. R1b rem|chunk guard only covers within-wu.

_chunk_size now prefers largest exact divisor <= target (no partial -> ring realigns per wu), R1b coarse+partial kept only as prime>target fallback (reachable only at 1 wu/core). Flagged 296->8 (win preserved). Failing shape 128->8/shrink->4 clean. Batching re-measured flat (reads still hidden) -> parked at per-tile default per refinement, scaffolding kept as live knob.

Flagged shape 9.666->9.006ms (1.073x, util 0.078->0.084) via coarsen 4->8. DM batching re-measured flat->parked. Golden 1181/1088/0-fail (regression fixed via prefer-divisor _chunk_size); unit 343/112-skip; --dev clean on coarse-chunk(22)+large-GQA(40)+flagged(1). Marked [~]: knob-turns exhausted (block L1/DEST-capped at 8), residual ~11x gap to 0.35 is structural (softmax phases dont overlap matmul). Filed R3c (overlap scheme-change).

plan: Append ones tile-column to V so PV matmul extra output column = rowsum(P); eliminates per-chunk row-sum reduce (~14%). Gated to bf16+fp32_dest_acc_en=False (flagged regime) => byte-identical elsewhere. Baseline 5.798ms warm median on flagged shape.

verdict: V-ones trick is a 15pct REGRESSION on flagged shape
rootcause: adds a full tile-column to the short-K PV matmul (25pct more PV work + 25pct wider O phases) to replace a cheap 1-wide row-sum reduce; the added tile-column FMA over K costs more than the dedicated reduce. Production SDPA avoids V-ones for exactly this reason (uses L1-accumulate-during-exp + 1-wide ones-vector matmul_reduce after loop).

verdict: V-ones lever measured 15pct regression (5.803->6.701ms, clock-controlled A/B), reverted; op byte-identical to R3c (green, perf 9/9)
followup: R3e: L1-accumulate-during-exp (raw-LLK dual-pack) - the correct row-sum elimination

detail: 4 cells fail: Q1x71x2048x64 MQA custom-mask fp32_dest=False (fused path), pcc~0.90 rms~0.43. All other 1177 pass. Discriminator: >1 work-unit-per-core (568wu/110cores) + custom mask + fused, untested regime.

text: Fused CB state (cb_row_sum partial / cb_sum_chunk / cb_corr) or init_bcast state carries wrong across work units when custom mask present. Reproduce with Q1x71x512x64 MQA custom fp32_dest=False (total_work=142>110).

Root cause: full init_bcast in fused Phase6 clobbered matmul persistent packer state (InitMode::Short does not fully re-issue pack cfg), drifting across chunks x work-units. Repro H71 S2048 custom rms 0.43.

Replaced full init_bcast with lightweight reconfig (reconfig_data_format + sub_bcast_cols_init_short + exp_tile_init + pack_reconfig_data_format), mirroring the eltwise_chain lowering. Repro rms 0.43->0.021, 0.17->0.021, 0.058->0.023. All pass.

detail: flagged 1x10x9472x128 same-session A/B: FUSE_OFF(reduce)=5.804ms (reproduces R3c 5.796ms), FUSED_ON(R3e)=5.440ms => 1.067x (6.27%, -364us). PCC>=0.997 held. Golden 1181/0, unit+precision 351 passed.
